info.title: dotnet-dev-certs-plus
info.description: Extended functionality for the dotnet dev-certs command, including machine store and WSL support.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Extended functionality for the dotnet dev-certs command

commands.name: https
commands.description: Manage the HTTPS development certificate with extended functionality

options.name: --store
options.description: Import cert to the specified store (machine)

options.name: --wsl
options.description: Import cert to WSL distro (default distro if no value specified)

options.name: --force
options.description: Skip confirmation prompt when cleaning [default: False]

options.name: --export-path
options.description: Full path to the exported certificate

options.name: --password
options.description: Password to use when exporting the certificate with the private key into a pfx file or to encrypt the Pem exported key

options.name: --no-password
options.description: Explicitly request that you don't use a password for the key when exporting a certificate to a PEM format [default: False]

options.name: --check
options.description: Check for the existence of the certificate but do not perform any action [default: False]

options.name: --clean
options.description: Cleans all HTTPS development certificates from the machine [default: False]

options.name: --import
options.description: Imports the provided HTTPS development certificate into the machine. All other HTTPS developer certificates will be cleared out

options.name: --format
options.description: Export the certificate in the given format. Valid values are Pfx and Pem. Pfx is the default.

options.name: --trust
options.description: Trust the certificate on the current platform [default: False]

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Display warnings and errors only [default: False]

options.name: --check-trust-machine-readable
options.description: Same as running --check --trust, but output the results in json [default: False]

options.name: --check-update
options.description: Check if a new version of the tool is available [default: False]
